# terrible_community_general_rhevm (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `password` (String) The password for user authentication.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `boot_order` (String) This option uses complex arguments and is a list of items that specify the bootorder.
- `cd_drive` (String) The CD you wish to have mounted on the VM when `state=cd`.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `cluster` (String) The RHEV/oVirt cluster in which you want you VM to start.
- `cpu_share` (Number) This parameter is used to configure the CPU share.
- `datacenter` (String) The RHEV/oVirt datacenter in which you want you VM to start.
- `del_prot` (Boolean) This option sets the delete protection checkbox.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `disks` (String) This option uses complex arguments and is a list of disks with the options `name`, `size`, and `domain`.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ifaces` (String) This option uses complex arguments and is a list of interfaces with the options `name` and `vlan`.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `image` (String) The template to use for the VM.
- `insecure_api` (Boolean) A boolean switch to make a secure or insecure connection to the server.
- `mempol` (Number) The minimum amount of memory you wish to reserve for this system.
- `name` (String) The name of the VM.
- `osver` (String) The operating system option in RHEV/oVirt.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `port` (Number) The port on which the API is reachable.
- `server` (String) The name/IP of your RHEV-m/oVirt instance.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) This serves to create/remove/update or powermanage your VM.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `type` (String) To define if the VM is a server or desktop.
- `user` (String) The user to authenticate with.
- `vm_ha` (Boolean) To make your VM High Available.
- `vmcpu` (Number) The number of CPUs you want in your VM.
- `vmhost` (String) The host you wish your VM to run on.
- `vmmem` (Number) The amount of memory you want your VM to use (in GB).

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `vm` (String) Returns all of the VMs variables and execution.
